title: Which Role Is Loudest Today? Which Is Starved?
subtitle: A journal practice for auditing the competing roles in your life and what each is receiving
excerpt: A journal practice for mapping the roles you carry and how much of you each one is currently receiving. For the chronic feeling of doing everything inadequately, as if your time and attention are spread too thin in every direction.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel fragmented across too many demands, or when you want to understand where your attention is going and what is being neglected.

content.text: A journal practice for auditing the competing roles. Most women carry several roles simultaneously: mother, partner, worker, friend, daughter, carer, neighbour, self. Each role has demands. Some are loud and urgent. Some are quiet and consistently underfunded. This practice names the roles and checks what each one is currently getting.
List every role you carry right now. Be thorough.
Include the invisible ones: the family historian, the one who remembers the birthdays, the person who manages the worry. Write every role, named or unnamed.
Which role is making the most noise this week?
The one with the most urgent demands, the most visible consequences, the one you can't ignore. Write what it is taking from you.
Which role is being systematically starved?

The one that goes quiet because it doesn't cause immediate consequences. Often: the self, the friendship, the creative interest, the physical health. Write what it has been asking for and not receiving.
What is one small allocation you could make to the starved role this week?
Not a full redistribution. One hour, one act, one conversation. Write what is possible and when.
